mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 8% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off behavior that drives S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and GDP growth signal a contraction in real economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns in contractionary regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05)

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% erodes real returns and increases discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in payroll employment and labor force participation signals weakening economic growth, driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or 60-day forward returns in contractionary regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05)

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with strong housing demand (HOUST) indicates expanding economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite positive signal

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal
